items.markdown:
A data pipeline is `a set of automated processes that moves raw information from one or more sources to a designated destination` (like a database or data warehouse). Along the way, it typically cleans, restructures, and enriches the data so it can be used for analytics, business intelligence, or machine learning.[](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/data-pipeline/) [[1]](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/data-pipeline/)[[2]](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/)[[3]](https://www.salesforce.com/data/connectivity/pipeline/)

The Three Core Components

Every data pipeline consists of three basic stages:[](https://hazelcast.com/foundations/event-driven-architecture/data-pipeline/) [[1]](https://hazelcast.com/foundations/event-driven-architecture/data-pipeline/)[[2]](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/)

1. **Source:** Where the data originates (e.g., website user logs, IoT sensors, CRM platforms, or mobile apps).[](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/data-pipeline/) [[1]](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/data-pipeline/)[[2]](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/)
2. **Processing/Transformation:** The steps taken to refine the data. This includes filtering out duplicates, standardizing formats (e.g., dates), validating accuracy, and masking sensitive information.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/data-pipeline) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/data-pipeline)[[2]](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/data-pipeline/)[[3]](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/)
3. **Destination (Sink):** The endpoint where the processed data is stored, such as a cloud data warehouse (for structured analysis) or a data lake (for raw/semi-structured data).[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/data-pipeline) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/data-pipeline)[[2]](https://www.databricks.com/blog/what-are-data-pipelines)[[3]](https://www.hpe.com/emea_africa/en/what-is/data-pipelines.html)

Common Processing Methods

- **Batch Processing:** Moving and processing chunks of data at scheduled intervals (e.g., processing all of yesterday's sales overnight).
- **Stream Processing:** Moving and analyzing data continuously in real-time as it is generated (e.g., real-time fraud detection for a credit card transaction or a live transit tracker).[](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/) [[1]](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/)[[2]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zNMiPL5nmt0)[[3]](https://estuary.dev/blog/data-integration-strategy/)[[4]](https://medium.com/@learn-devops-cloud/real-time-examples-of-batch-processing-and-stream-processing-5f19226197c4)[[5]](https://www.salesforce.com/data/connectivity/pipeline/)

Pipeline vs. ETL/ELT While often used interchangeably, "data pipeline" is an umbrella term. An **ETL** (Extract, Transform, Load) or **ELT** (Extract, Load, Transform) pipeline is simply a *specific type* of data pipeline designed to prepare data specifically for storage and analysis.[](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/) [[1]](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/)[[2]](https://www.salesforce.com/data/connectivity/pipeline/)Why Are They Essential?Raw data collected from various applications is largely unusable until it is organized. Data pipelines eliminate manual data entry, prevent data silos, and ensure that the information feeding into operational decisions, executive dashboards, and AI models is accurate and reliable.[](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/data-pipeline/) [[1]](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/data-pipeline/)[[2]](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/)To explore building or managing modern workflows, check out the comprehensive guides on [![](https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/faviconV2?url=https://aws.amazon.com&client=AIM&size=128&type=FAVICON&fallback_opts=TYPE,SIZE,URL)
⁠AWS What is Data Pipeline](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/data-pipeline/) or [![](https://encrypted-tbn2.gstatic.com/faviconV2?url=https://www.ibm.com&client=AIM&size=128&type=FAVICON&fallback_opts=TYPE,SIZE,URL)
⁠IBM What is a Data Pipeline](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/data-pipeline).[[1]](https://www.bmc.com/blogs/data-pipeline/)

items.title: What is meant by data pipeline?

expanded_element.title: What Is a Data Pipeline? | IBM
expanded_element.description: A data pipeline is a method in which raw data is ingested from various data sources, transformed and then ported to a data store, such as a data lake or data warehouse, for analysis. Before data flows into a data repository, it usually undergoes some data processing.

expanded_element.title: What is a Data Pipeline? Types, Components and Architecture | Hevo
expanded_element.description: A data pipeline architecture provides a complete blueprint of the processes and technologies used to replicate data from a source to a destination system, including data extraction, transformation, and loading.
